title: Water Purification
summary: How to make water safe to drink using five methods, ranked from simplest to most effective. Contaminated water kills faster than most threats you will face.

title: Fire Making
summary: Fire provides heat, light, cooked food, sterilized water, protection, and the psychological anchor of civilization. Four methods from friction to prepared tinder.

title: Shelter Construction
summary: Shelter protects from weather, wind, cold, and predators — often the difference between survival and hypothermia. Five methods ranked by time, materials, and durability.

title: Food Preservation: Extending Survival Through Storage
summary: Food spoils. Preservation methods—drying, salt-curing, fermenting, smoking, cool storage—stop spoilage and create nutrient-dense foods that sustain you through seasons when fresh food is scarce.

title: Hunting & Trapping
summary: Passive trap construction and active hunting techniques to acquire protein—from deadfall traps to field dressing.

title: Fishing
summary: Fish are protein-rich, renewable, and accessible even without modern tools. Hand-lines, weirs, spears, and traps catch food in hours, not days. Learn to read water, place gear, and harvest without depleting.

title: Basic First Aid & Wound Care
summary: Stop bleeding, clean wounds, treat burns and fractures without modern equipment. These are the skills that keep you alive in the first days after collapse.

title: Rope & Knots: Essential Lashing and Binding Techniques
summary: Rope holds your shelter together, secures your cargo, creates traps, and saves your life. Master ten essential knots and you can build, bind, and survive.

title: Primitive Tool Making
summary: Bone, wood, and soft stone tools are faster and often better than flint. Make needles, scrapers, fish hooks, digging sticks, and grinding stones from readily available materials.

title: Navigation Without GPS
summary: Find direction and keep your bearing using only the sun, stars, terrain, and natural landmarks. Navigation keeps you from walking in circles when you are lost.

title: Observation and Measurement: Building the Foundation of Science
summary: Science starts with careful observation and measurement. Learn to observe systematically, measure accurately, record reliably, and estimate uncertainty.

title: Arithmetic: Counting, Calculation, and Practical Numbers
summary: Master addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. Learn to calculate without paper, estimate quickly, and verify results. Arithmetic is the foundation of all practical mathematics.

title: Geometry
summary: Practical geometry: measuring angles, calculating areas and volumes, using triangulation for surveying, and building straight walls and true angles.

title: Trigonometry
summary: Using trigonometry to find distances and heights: sine, cosine, tangent, and practical applications for surveying and navigation.

title: Analytic Geometry
summary: Using coordinate systems to map land and locate positions: coordinate grids, distance calculation, slope, and practical surveying applications.

title: Calculus Concepts
summary: Practical calculus concepts: rates of change, optimization, and accumulation. Foundation for engineering and resource management.

title: Atomic Theory: Understanding Matter and Elements
summary: All matter is made of atoms. Understanding atoms explains why materials behave as they do and how to work with them for survival.

title: Chemical Reactions: Making and Breaking Bonds
summary: Chemical reactions rearrange atoms, releasing or absorbing energy. Understanding reactions helps you control fire, preserve food, produce materials, and avoid hazards.

title: Acids and Bases: Properties, Reactions, and Practical Applications
summary: Acids and bases are opposites that neutralize each other. Understanding them helps you preserve food, make soap, manage soil pH, and avoid chemical hazards.

title: Electrochemistry: Electricity from Chemistry, Chemistry from Electricity
summary: Chemical reactions can produce electricity, and electricity can drive chemical reactions. Understanding this helps you generate power, refine metals, and protect against corrosion.

title: Making Soap: Chemistry, Process, and Practical Application
summary: Soap is made by reacting fats with bases (lye). Understanding soap chemistry helps you produce hygiene products essential for health and survival.

title: Glassmaking Chemistry: Creating Transparent Materials
summary: Glass is made by heating silica with fluxes and stabilizers. Understanding glassmaking helps you produce containers, windows, and optical instruments.

title: Producing Sulfuric Acid: Industrial Chemistry at Scale
summary: Sulfuric acid is the most important industrial chemical. Understanding its production helps you process metals, treat water, and manufacture other chemicals.

title: Producing Fertilizers: Sustaining Agriculture
summary: Plant growth depends on nutrients (nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium). Understanding fertilizer production helps you sustain high agricultural yields post-collapse.

title: Organic Chemistry Basics: Understanding Carbon Compounds
summary: Organic chemistry is the chemistry of carbon compounds. Understanding organic chemistry helps you produce alcohol, preserve food, and work with biological materials.

title: Mechanics: Motion, Forces, and Simple Machines
summary: Mechanics is the study of motion and forces. Understanding mechanics helps you use tools effectively, design structures, and create useful machines.

title: Heat and Thermodynamics: Energy Transformation
summary: Thermodynamics is the study of heat and its transformation. Understanding heat helps you manage fire, generate power, and use energy efficiently.

title: Waves and Sound: Understanding Vibration and Propagation
summary: Sound is a wave; understanding waves helps you communicate, navigate, and predict how sound behaves in different environments.

title: Optics: Light, Lenses, and Vision
summary: Light is a wave and a particle. Understanding optics helps you make lenses, correct vision, and create optical instruments.

title: Static Electricity: Charge, Force, and Lightning
summary: Static electricity is the buildup of electric charge. Understanding it helps you predict and protect against lightning, understand electrical phenomena, and work with electrical systems.

title: Magnetism and Electromagnetism: Fields and Motion
summary: Magnetism is created by moving electrical charges. Understanding magnetism helps you generate electricity, power motors, and navigate using compass.

title: Electromagnetism: Unified Theory of Electricity and Magnetism
summary: Electricity and magnetism are two aspects of one phenomenon. Understanding electromagnetism helps you work with electromagnetic waves and understand energy transmission.

title: Nuclear Physics Basics: Understanding the Nucleus
summary: The nucleus contains most of an atom's mass. Understanding nuclear physics helps you understand energy sources, radiation safety, and detection.

title: Cell Theory: Understanding Life at Its Foundation
summary: All life is made of cells. Cells are the smallest units of life. This chapter explains the three core principles and why they matter for food, medicine, and survival.

title: Genetics Basics: Heredity, Variation, and Mutation
summary: Traits pass from parent to offspring through genetic material. Understanding inheritance helps you breed crops and animals, predict disease, and understand why populations change.

title: Evolution and Natural Selection: How Populations Change
summary: Populations change over generations through natural selection. Understanding this explains disease resistance, crop failure, and how organisms adapt to collapse scenarios.

title: Ecology: Energy, Populations, and Ecosystems
summary: Organisms live in systems where energy flows and populations interact. Understanding ecosystems helps you manage resources sustainably and predict population changes.

title: Human Anatomy
summary: Essential human anatomy: skeletal system, organs, blood vessels, and nervous system. Foundation for medical diagnosis and treatment.

title: Microbiology: Bacteria, Viruses, and Microscopic Life
summary: Invisible organisms cause disease, spoil food, and enable fermentation. Understanding microorganisms helps you prevent disease, preserve food, and manage health.

title: Taxonomy: Classifying and Identifying Organisms
summary: Organisms are classified in a hierarchical system. Understanding classification helps you identify organisms, understand relationships, and make decisions about what is safe to eat or dangerous to avoid.

title: Botany in Depth: Plant Structure, Function, and Cultivation
summary: Plants are the foundation of post-collapse survival. Understanding plant structure, reproduction, and growth helps you grow food, identify plants, and maximize productivity.

title: Soil Science
summary: Healthy soil grows healthy crops. Learn soil types, fertility assessment, amendments, and management techniques. Post-collapse agriculture depends on understanding your soil.

title: Crop Selection
summary: Choose crops that match your climate, soil, and labor capacity. The goal is calories first, then nutrition, then surplus. Select for reliability over novelty.

title: Seed Saving
summary: Save seed from this year's harvest for next year's planting. Seed saving ensures independence from external sources and creates locally-adapted varieties over generations.

title: Irrigation
summary: Grow crops where water is scarce or unreliable by building irrigation systems. Gravity-fed channels are simplest; learn surface, furrow, and drip irrigation methods.

title: Crop Rotation
summary: Rotate crops annually to maintain soil fertility, prevent pest buildup, and break disease cycles. A good rotation plan feeds your settlement indefinitely without depleting soil.

title: Composting at Scale
summary: Build large composting systems that turn crop residue, manure, and kitchen waste into soil amendment. Hot compost, windrows, and vermicompost each serve different scales and climates.

title: Pest Control
summary: Identifying agricultural pests, non-chemical control methods, crop rotation, and integrated pest management without industrial pesticides.

title: Livestock Integration
summary: Integrate livestock into your farm system. Animals provide draft power, manure, food, and fiber. Well-managed animals multiply the productivity of your land.

title: Geology Basics
summary: Understand rocks, minerals, and geological structures. Geology reveals where resources are located. Post-collapse survival depends on reading the land.

title: Ore Identification
summary: How to identify 20 economically important ores in the field using visual characteristics, streak tests, hardness tests, and acid reactivity without laboratory equipment.

title: Groundwater Finding
summary: How to locate groundwater using geology and field observation, not dowsing rods. Understanding aquifers, springs, seeps, and artesian wells allows you to find water reliably without luck.

title: Coal and Peat
summary: How to identify coal and peat deposits, extract them sustainably, and convert coal into coke for metalworking—the foundation of heavy industry.

title: Anatomy Overview: Understanding the Human Body
summary: You cannot treat what you do not understand. This chapter maps the major systems of the human body—skeletal, muscular, cardiovascular, respiratory, nervous, digestive, and reproductive—so you can recognize injury, infection, and disease.

title: Wound Care
summary: Step-by-step wound cleaning, closure, and infection prevention using available materials and no modern equipment.

title: Germ Theory in Practice: Sterilization Without Autoclave
summary: Sterilize medical instruments and environments without electricity or autoclaves. The goal is making tools and spaces safe enough that wounds heal, not become infected.

title: Fever and Infection Management
summary: Fever is a defense mechanism. Recognize the difference between controlled fever (healing) and runaway fever (sepsis). Manage infection, prevent complications, and know when a patient is past saving.

title: Fractures & Dislocations: Recognizing and Managing Broken Bones
summary: Broken bones and dislocated joints are common in survival situations. Recognizing the type of fracture and immobilizing it properly prevents disability and life-threatening complications like fat embolism or compartment syndrome.

title: Childbirth: Assisting Pregnancy and Delivery
summary: Pregnancy and childbirth are normal processes but carry risks, especially without modern medical care. Understanding labor, recognizing complications, and assisting delivery ensures both mother and baby survive.

title: Field Surgery
summary: When you must cut to save a life: amputation, abscess drainage, and emergency surgery without anesthesia or modern equipment. Last-resort interventions that separate survivors from corpses.

title: Herbal Pharmacopoeia: 40+ Medicinal Plants
summary: Fifty medicinal plants with their active compounds, preparation methods, dosages, and interactions. This is your post-collapse pharmacopoeia.

title: The Celestial Sphere: Understanding the Night Sky
summary: The night sky appears as a sphere surrounding Earth. Understanding this celestial sphere helps you navigate, predict seasons, and locate celestial objects.

title: Star Identification: Finding and Reading the Sky
summary: The night sky contains recognizable patterns. Learning to identify key stars and constellations helps you navigate, track time, and understand the cosmos.

title: Latitude from Observation: Finding Your Position
summary: Celestial objects' positions reveal your latitude. Understanding this helps you find your location without maps or instruments—or with simple handmade tools.

title: Property Systems: Commons vs. Private Ownership
summary: Define who owns what and how disputes are resolved. Commons (shared resources) and private property both work; the failure mode is ambiguity.

title: Rule of Law: Criminal and Civil Justice Without Lawyers
summary: Establish consistent rules for crime and civil disputes. Justice without lawyers means clear rules, community judges, and enforceable penalties.

title: Government Structures: Organizing Decision-Making Without Professionals
summary: Design a government structure that makes binding decisions without professional politicians. Options: assemblies, councils, rotating magistrates. The failure mode is concentration of power.

title: Taxation and Labor
summary: How to fund collective projects (defense, infrastructure, administration) through fair taxation systems that do not bankrupt individuals or create resentment.

title: Record-Keeping and Archives: Preserving Information for the Future
summary: Keep written records of property, laws, decisions, and knowledge. Without archives, history is forgotten, disputes return, and civilizations restart from scratch.

title: Currency from Scratch: Creating Money Without Central Banks
summary: Establish a medium of exchange to replace barter. Options: commodity-based (grain, metal), token-based (coins, tokens), or credit-based (ledgers). Currency enables specialization and long-term planning.

title: How to Use This Guide
summary: Guide structure, learning paths, how to find information, and recommended reading orders for different situations.

title: The 16-Step Progression
summary: 16 stages of civilization development: from survival to advanced society. Know what stage you are at and what comes next.

title: Failure Modes
summary: How civilizations historically fail, the warning signs to watch for, and the structural vulnerabilities that cause collapse.

title: Knowledge Preservation
summary: How to keep critical technical and survival information alive through copying, teaching, and reliable archiving systems that survive material decay and loss of educated people.

title: Nutrition Requirements
summary: Understand human nutritional requirements post-collapse. Adequate nutrition prevents disease and maintains strength for physical labor. Know caloric needs, macronutrient ratios, and essential micronutrients.

title: Lacto-Fermentation
summary: Ferment vegetables using salt and time. Lacto-fermentation preserves vitamin C, creates probiotics, and requires no heat or special equipment. Essential post-collapse vegetable preservation.

title: Brewing
summary: Brew beer and mead. Fermented beverages preserve grain and honey, provide calories, and were historical staples. Beer was safer than water pre-pasteurization. Post-collapse, brewing provides preserved nutrition and safe storage.

title: Vinegar Production
summary: Make vinegar from fruit, grain, or wine. Vinegar preserves vegetables, flavors food, and has medicinal properties. Production requires only time and oxygen (opposite of beer brewing).

title: Baking and Leavening
summary: Make leavened bread post-collapse using sourdough starter or wild yeast. Fermented bread improves digestibility, texture, and nutrition. Master sourdough for staple grain conversion.

title: Dairy Processing
summary: Process fresh milk into preserved products. Cheese, butter, and yogurt extend milk's shelf life from days to months/years. Essential for converting perishable milk into stable storage.

title: Salt Curing & Smoking
summary: Preserve meat and fish using salt and smoke. These ancient methods eliminate water and pathogens, preserving protein for months/years. Essential post-collapse meat storage.

title: Spoilage Science
summary: Understand what spoils food and how to prevent it. Bacteria, mold, oxidation, and enzyme action destroy food value. Know detection methods and prevention strategies.

title: Plant Fiber Processing: From Plant to Usable Fiber
summary: Plant fibers are the foundation of textile production. This chapter explains how to extract, prepare, and process fibers from common plants like flax, hemp, nettles, and grasses. Mastering these techniques enables rope-making, weaving, and fabric production in a post-collapse world where synthetic fibers are unavailable.

title: Animal Fiber Processing: Wool, Hair, and Silk
summary: Animal fibers—wool, hair, and silk—produce some of the finest, warmest, and most durable textiles. This chapter explains how to process fiber from sheep, goats, other animals, and silkworms. Mastering these techniques provides clothing superior to plant fibers in warmth and durability, and creates valuable trade goods.

title: Spinning: Converting Fiber to Thread and Yarn
summary: Spinning converts prepared fibers into thread and yarn suitable for weaving, knitting, and other textile work. This chapter explains the principles of twist, ply, and yarn structure, then teaches hand spinning using simple tools. Mastering spinning is essential for converting raw materials into finished textiles.

title: Loom Construction: Building Weaving Equipment
summary: A loom is a device that holds warp threads taut while weft threads are passed across them to create cloth. This chapter explains how looms work, describes different loom types, and provides step-by-step instructions for constructing functional looms from simple materials. Building your own loom enables textile production without dependence on external suppliers.

title: Trauma and Crisis: Recognition and Psychological First Aid
summary: During collapse and after, people experience severe trauma. Recognizing acute stress responses and providing psychological first aid prevents psychological breakdown and preserves group functioning.

title: Group Dynamics
summary: How groups form, achieve cohesion, differentiate roles, and manage in-group vs. out-group boundaries.

title: Conflict Resolution: Mediation, Negotiation, and Restorative Justice
summary: When two people or groups conflict, an untrained mediator often escalates the dispute into violence. Principled negotiation techniques, restorative justice, and structured mediation transform conflict into understanding and agreements.

title: Child Development: Stages, Needs, and Growth
summary: Children develop through predictable stages, with different cognitive, emotional, and physical abilities at each age. Meeting needs appropriate to a child's stage prevents developmental delay and psychological harm.

title: Learning Theory: How Humans Learn and Retain Knowledge
summary: Knowing how memory works and how learning is retained allows teachers and learners to study efficiently, master skills faster, and avoid wasting effort on ineffective methods.

title: School Design: Building Effective Education Systems
summary: Effective schools are not replicas of pre-collapse institutions. Post-collapse schools must adapt to community needs, mixed-age groups, limited resources, and practical skill requirements while preserving academic knowledge.

title: Oral Tradition: Preserving Knowledge Through Stories and Memory Techniques
summary: Before writing, humans preserved knowledge through oral tradition—memorized stories, poems, and mnemonics. Mastering these techniques ensures knowledge survives even without writing.

title: Ethical Frameworks
summary: Three ethical frameworks (consequentialism, deontology, virtue ethics) and how to apply them when civilization-building requires impossible choices.

title: Justice and Rights
summary: Building fair legal systems and defining rights when civilization restarts from collapse.

title: Resource Allocation
summary: How to distribute food, medicine, labor, and other critical resources fairly under scarcity when you cannot give everyone everything they need.

title: Social Contracts
summary: Writing founding documents, charters, and constitutions that establish how a post-collapse community governs itself.

title: Decision Under Uncertainty
summary: Using Bayesian reasoning and expected value to make decisions when outcomes are uncertain.

title: Leadership and Authority
summary: Sources of legitimate authority, how to lead without tyranny, and how to plan succession.

title: Soil Conservation
summary: Prevent soil loss and maintain soil structure. Erosion destroys productivity within years. Conservation practices sustain agriculture for generations post-collapse.

title: Water Catchment
summary: Capture and store rainfall. Post-collapse water security depends on effective catchment systems. Cisterns, ponds, and springs enable agriculture during dry seasons.

title: Forest Management
summary: Manage forests for food, fuel, and timber without depletion. Sustainable harvesting maintains forest productivity for generations. Post-collapse civilization depends on forest resources.

title: Carrying Capacity
summary: Calculate how many people your land can support. Carrying capacity determines settlement viability. Exceed it, and collapse is certain.

title: Ecosystem Restoration
summary: Restore degraded ecosystems to productivity and health. Restoration rebuilds soil, water, biodiversity, and climate resilience. Post-collapse survival depends on restoring productive land.

title: Pollution Prevention
summary: Prevent pollution of water, soil, and food. Post-collapse settlements have no environmental agencies. Pollution control depends on knowledge and prevention. Contamination means illness, starvation, or death.

title: Fortification Design
summary: Design defensive structures that deter attackers without consuming unsustainable labor. Learn to site fortifications, size walls and towers, integrate water barriers, and account for terrain advantages.

title: Perimeter Security
summary: Organize effective guard rotations, set up alarm systems, and monitor perimeter threats without burning out your defenders. Perimeter security is as much about routine and communication as about weapons.

title: Defensive Strategy
summary: Plan how to defend your settlement if attacked. Strategy is how you use your fortifications, terrain, and people. Effective defense often means avoiding direct combat, not winning it.

title: Siege Basics
summary: When your settlement is surrounded and supply lines are cut, you are under siege. Survive by managing supplies, water, and morale. Know when to fight for a breakout, when to negotiate, and when to surrender.

title: Weapon Basics
summary: Learn to make and use defensive weapons: bows, spears, and simple crossbows. Focus is on simplicity and defensive value, not dominance. Post-collapse weapons are tools, not status.

title: Lessons from History
summary: Study how civilizations collapsed: Rome, Byzantium, Easter Island. Learn the patterns of military overextension, defensive failure, and resource depletion. Your settlement must not repeat these mistakes.

title: Cattle and Draft Animals
summary: Understand cattle anatomy, recognize disease signs, treat common illnesses, and work draft animals safely. Healthy cattle are the foundation of post-collapse agriculture.

title: Pigs and Poultry
summary: Manage pig and poultry health. Recognize common diseases, prevent parasite infestations, and keep small livestock productive. Pigs and chickens are efficient converters of scraps to food.

title: Goats and Sheep
summary: Manage goat and sheep health. Both are hardy, productive animals. Recognize breed-specific issues, prevent parasites, and maintain reproductive health. These are ideal secondary livestock post-collapse.

title: Field Surgery
summary: Perform basic surgical procedures on livestock: castration, dehorning, castration, and wound care. These procedures improve animal welfare and productivity.

title: Parasite Control
summary: Manage parasite infestations in livestock. Parasites cause poor growth, anemia, disease, and death. Prevention (rotation, sanitation) is far more effective than treatment post-collapse.

title: Quarantine Protocols
summary: Quarantine new and sick animals. Isolation is the most powerful tool post-collapse to prevent disease spread. A 2-4 week isolation period catches most infections before they enter your herd.

title: Tracking and Sign
summary: Read tracks, scat, browse, and runs to locate animals, predict their movement, and understand their behavior. Tracking turns a forest into readable text.

title: Terrain Navigation
summary: Read the landscape to navigate reliably: identify ridges, valleys, water flows, and distinctive features. Terrain never lies if you know how to read it.

title: Edible Plants in Depth
summary: Field identification and preparation of 30+ edible plants by species, with toxin removal and seasonal timing.

title: Primitive Tools (Flint Knapping)
summary: Flint, chert, and obsidian can be worked into the sharpest cutting edges in the world. Learn to identify knappable stone and produce blades, points, and scrapers in hours without modern tools.

title: Hunting Strategies: Ambush, Drives, and Snare Placement
summary: Kill game reliably by understanding where animals go, what they fear, and how to intercept them. Three core strategies: ambush, drive, and snare placement.

title: Shelter by Environment: Desert, Arctic, Temperate, Jungle
summary: Build shelters matched to your climate: desert, arctic, temperate forest, or jungle. Material selection and design determine survival.

title: Emergency Signaling
summary: Signal for rescue using mirrors, smoke, ground signals, and sound. Know what rescuers see and hear, and how to make your signal unmistakable.

title: Latrine Design: Pit, Vault, and VIP Systems
summary: A latrine safely contains human waste in a way that protects groundwater, prevents smell, and works in various soil and water table conditions.

title: Sewage Treatment: Wastewater Processing Without Sewer Lines
summary: Large settlements produce waste faster than latrines can handle. Sewage treatment systems use biology to break down waste and remove pathogens, allowing treated water to be reused or safely returned to the environment.

title: Composting Toilets: Design and Operation
summary: A composting toilet converts human waste into safe, nutrient-rich soil amendment. No water, no sewage pipes, no smell — just controlled decomposition that produces fertilizer.

title: Water Testing: Field Methods for Contamination Detection
summary: Test water for contamination without a lab. Detect bacterial, chemical, and physical hazards using improvised methods.

title: Vector Control: Mosquitoes, Rats, Flies Without Pesticides
summary: Control disease vectors (mosquitoes, rats, flies) through environmental management, physical barriers, and biological methods. Prevention is more effective than treatment.

title: Waste Management and Hazardous Waste Isolation
summary: Separate solid waste into organic (compostable), inert (landfill), and hazardous (isolated). Know what decomposes, what stays forever, and what poisons the land.

title: Greywater Systems: Infiltration Trenches, Reed Beds & Reuse
summary: Treat and reuse greywater (water from sinks, showers, laundry) through natural systems. Conserve freshwater while preventing pollution.

title: Aspirin Synthesis: Producing a Essential Pain Reliever
summary: Aspirin is one of the oldest and most useful drugs. Understanding how to synthesize it from salicylic acid helps you produce essential medicine post-collapse.

title: Antiseptics: Preventing and Treating Infections
summary: Antiseptics kill or inhibit microorganisms on skin and surfaces. Producing and using antiseptics is critical for post-collapse infection prevention.

title: Silicon from Sand: Carbothermic Reduction
summary: The complete process for reducing silicon dioxide (quartz sand) to metallurgical-grade silicon using carbon at 1700°C+. This is Step 1 of 12 in the path from beach sand to working transistors.

title: Silicon Purification: Zone Refining and the Siemens Process
summary: Two purification paths from 99% to 99.9999999% pure silicon: the industrial Siemens process (trichlorosilane decomposition) and the laboratory zone refining method. Both are explained from first principles.

title: Magnetic Core Memory: Theory, Construction, and Operation
summary: Magnetic core memory — used in every early mainframe, NASA Apollo computer, and first-generation satellite systems — stores one bit per tiny ferrite ring. This chapter explains the physics, construction of memory planes, and the coincident-current addressing scheme from scratch.
